pattern: Numbers group into bands of equal digit-length (1-9, 10-99, 100-999, ...), so subtracting whole bands locates which number holds the target position, and a final division and remainder pick the exact digit without ever building the sequence.
hints: The sequence has 9 one-digit numbers, 90 two-digit numbers, 900 three-digit numbers, and so on; each band contributes a known count of digits.
Skip whole bands by subtracting their total digit count from n until n falls inside the current band of d-digit numbers.
Within the band, (n-1) divided by d names which number you are in and (n-1) mod d names which digit of that number to return.

edge_inputs.description: position lands inside a two-digit number on a zero digit
first position of the whole sequence
last digit of the single-digit band
first digit of the two-digit band, the leading 1 of 10
last digit of the two-digit band, the 9 of 99
first digit of the three-digit band, the 1 of 100

stress.description: positions spread across the full legal range so multiple digit bands are skipped
stress.note: wide range forces band-skipping arithmetic for large n

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def findNthDigit(self, n):
        # independent derivation: accumulate band sizes, then extract the digit
        # by repeated integer division from the right (no string indexing).
        d = 1
        first = 1
        while True:
            band_numbers = 9 * first
            band_digits = band_numbers * d
            if n <= band_digits:
                break
            n -= band_digits
            d += 1
            first *= 10
        idx = n - 1
        number = first + idx // d
        pos_from_left = idx % d
        pos_from_right = d - 1 - pos_from_left
        for _ in range(pos_from_right):
            number //= 10
        return number % 10
